Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family, COMT subfamily
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family. COMT subfamily. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01020}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=isoliquiritigenin + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 2'-O-methylisoliquiritigenin + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:21608, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:310312, ChEBI:CHEBI:519567; EC=2.1.1.154; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:1731632, ECO:0000269|PubMed:9055445}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=licodione + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 2'-O-methyllicodione + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:18521, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:77642, ChEBI:CHEBI:77711; EC=2.1.1.65; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:9055445};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Methylates the 2'-hydroxyl of isoliquiritigenin and licodione. Does not methylate narigenin chalcone, caffeic acid or daidzein. Involved in the root nodulation initiation by promoting the biosynthesis of nod-inducing molecules. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:1731632, ECO:0000269|PubMed:9055445}.
